title: Exercises: Motional Modes
description: Practice normal mode calculations for ion chains

exercises.title: Two-Ion Normal Modes
exercises.description: Calculate and visualize the normal modes of a two-ion chain.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def two_ion_modes(omega_z_MHz: float) -> dict:
    """Calculate two-ion normal mode frequencies and eigenvectors."""
    # COM mode: both ions move together
    omega_com = omega_z_MHz
    v_com = np.array([1, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
    
    # Stretch mode: ions move oppositely
    omega_stretch = # Your calculation
    v_stretch = # Your calculation
    
    return {
        'omega_com_MHz': omega_com,
        'omega_stretch_MHz': omega_stretch,
        'v_com': v_com,
        'v_stretch': v_stretch
    }

modes = two_ion_modes(1.0)
print(f"COM frequency: {modes['omega_com_MHz']:.3f} MHz")
print(f"Stretch frequency: {modes['omega_stretch_MHz']:.3f} MHz")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def two_ion_modes(omega_z_MHz):
    omega_com = omega_z_MHz
    v_com = np.array([1, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
    
    omega_stretch = np.sqrt(3) * omega_z_MHz
    v_stretch = np.array([1, -1]) / np.sqrt(2)
    
    return {
        'omega_com_MHz': omega_com,
        'omega_stretch_MHz': omega_stretch,
        'v_com': v_com,
        'v_stretch': v_stretch
    }

modes = two_ion_modes(1.0)
print(f"COM: {modes['omega_com_MHz']:.3f} MHz, mode = {modes['v_com']}")
print(f"Stretch: {modes['omega_stretch_MHz']:.3f} MHz, mode = {modes['v_stretch']}")
hints: Stretch mode has ions moving oppositely

exercises.title: Lamb-Dicke Parameter
exercises.description: Calculate the Lamb-Dicke parameter for Ca+ with 729 nm laser and 1 MHz trap frequency.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

hbar = 1.055e-34
mass_Ca = 40 * 1.66e-27
wavelength = 729e-9
omega = 1e6 * 2 * np.pi

k = 2 * np.pi / wavelength
eta = # Your calculation

print(f"Lamb-Dicke parameter: {eta:.3f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

hbar = 1.055e-34
mass_Ca = 40 * 1.66e-27
wavelength = 729e-9
omega = 1e6 * 2 * np.pi

k = 2 * np.pi / wavelength
z_zpf = np.sqrt(hbar / (2 * mass_Ca * omega))  # Zero-point fluctuation
eta = k * z_zpf

print(f"Zero-point motion: {z_zpf*1e9:.2f} nm")
print(f"Lamb-Dicke parameter: {eta:.3f}")
print(f"This is in the Lamb-Dicke regime (eta << 1)")
